evidence: All 52 tests pass: 15 internal unit tests + 37 integration tests, including two new tests (`codex_nonzero_exit_moves_temp_files_to_runtime_failed`, `nonzero_exit_writes_failed_raw_output_for_claude`).

All three non-zero-exit error paths in `invoke()` call `preserve_failure_artifacts()` before `cleanup()`: stale-session path (line 859), stale-session retry failure (line 886-888), and main non-zero exit (line 910).
`cleanup()` correctly short-circuits when `invocation_failed` is `true` (line 53-55), preventing deletion of preserved artifacts.
Spawn-failure paths (lines 845, 875) correctly skip `preserve_failure_artifacts` since there is no child output to preserve, and cleanup runs normally to remove temp files.
`best_effort_move_file` handles cross-filesystem moves via copy+delete fallback, and silently ignores NotFound errors.
`format_failed_raw_output` produces well-structured output with `=== stdout ===` and `=== stderr ===` sections, verified by test assertions.
New `invocation_failed: Arc<AtomicBool>` field added to `PreparedCommand` struct and initialized at all construction sites (Claude and Codex prepare paths).
